title: Celebrating a Decade of styled-components
author: Evan Jacobs
description: Ten years of visual primitives for the component age. A look back at where we've been, where we are, and where we're headed.

title: Why styled-components?
author: Evan Jacobs
description: A look at what sets styled-components apart from other CSS-in-JS libraries, from developer experience and performance to the power of staying close to CSS.

title: How we built a component library that people actually enjoy using
author: Chase McCoy
description: Lessons from building Sprout Social's Seeds design system — how great tooling, documentation, and developer empathy create component libraries people actually want to use.

title: Announcing styled-components v5: Beast Mode
author: Evan Jacobs
description: styled-components v5 delivers 50% faster server-side rendering, 22% smaller bundles, and RTL support with no breaking changes.

title: Build Better Component Libraries with Styled System
author: Alan B Smith
description: How Styled System brings constraint-based design to React component libraries, making it easier to build consistent, responsive UIs with props instead of custom CSS.

title: Releasing support for the css prop in create-react-app
author: Max Stoiber
description: The styled-components css prop now works out of the box with Create React App, no eject or babel config needed.

title: Announcing native support for the css prop in styled-components
author: Max Stoiber
description: Introducing first-class support for the css prop in styled-components, enabling inline styling with full theming and interpolation support.

title: Releasing styled-components v4 final
author: Evan Jacobs
description: styled-components v4 is officially stable after beta testing, bringing a smaller bundle, the new createGlobalStyle API, and full React 16 StrictMode compliance.

title: Announcing styled-components v4: Better, Faster, Stronger
author: Evan Jacobs
description: A deep dive into styled-components v4: a brand new global styles API, native \

title: How styled-components works: A deep dive under the hood
author: Eugene Gluhotorenko
description: A technical deep dive into how styled-components processes tagged template literals, generates CSS, and injects styles into the DOM.

title: How to build a simple HackerNews feed with styled-components
author: Andreas Reiterer
description: A hands-on tutorial building a HackerNews feed clone to learn the fundamentals of styled-components, from basic components to the attrs helper.

title: styled-components getting started
author: Scott Spence
description: A beginner-friendly walkthrough of styling a Create React App project with styled-components, covering theming, global styles, and component extension.

title: v3.1.0: A massive performance boost and streaming server-side rendering support
author: Evan Jacobs
description: v3.1.0 introduces insertRule-based CSS injection for up to 10-20x faster rendering in production, plus streaming server-side rendering support for React 16.

title: Getting Sassy with styled-components
author: Adam Gruber
description: A practical guide to incrementally migrating Sass-based apps to styled-components using ThemeProvider and the sass-extract toolchain.

title: Responsive Styled Components in 3 Minutes
author: Matt Granmoe
description: Build a declarative responsive component system in just 18 lines of code using styled-components and JavaScript's reduce function.

title: Building a Blog With Next.js and styled-components
author: Zach Sherman
description: A step-by-step tutorial for building a universal blog application with Next.js, styled-components, and Express.

title: How to create responsive UI with styled-components
author: Andreas Reiterer
description: Learn to build a responsive 12-column grid system from scratch using styled-components and CSS media queries, no framework required.

title: Announcing styled-components v2: A smaller, faster drop-in upgrade
author: Max Stoiber
description: styled-components v2 ships with a 45% smaller bundle, best-in-class server-side rendering, and new API helpers like .attrs, .extend, and .withComponent.
